output:
Looking at this, I need to compare P(large feet | college degree or higher) vs P(large feet | high school degree or lower).

From the given data:

- P(college degree or higher) = 36%, so P(high school degree or lower) = 64%
- P(high school degree or lower AND large feet) = 43%
- P(college degree or higher AND large feet) = 10%

**Conditional probabilities:**

- P(large feet | college degree or higher) = 10% / 36% ≈ **27.8%**
- P(large feet | high school degree or lower) = 43% / 64% ≈ **67.2%**

**No.** The chance of large feet is **smaller** when observing college degree or higher (27.8%) compared to high school degree or lower (67.2%).
